description: Summer season crops requiring irrigation, grown between Kharif and Rabi seasons

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Zaid Maize
steps: Select well-drained sandy loam to loamy soil with pH 6.0–7.5.
Deep plough with disc plough to 25-30 cm depth to break hardpan.
Apply 10 tonnes/hectare of well-decomposed FYM (farmyard manure) and incorporate.
Cross harrow 2-3 times to achieve fine tilth; use cultivator followed by planking.
Form ridges and furrows at 60-75 cm row spacing for better irrigation management.
Ensure no waterlogging — maize is highly sensitive to standing water.

step_2_seed_selection_and_treatment.title: Seed Selection & Treatment
steps: Use short-duration hybrid varieties suitable for summer: HQPM-1, PEHM-2, DHM-117, Vivek QPM-9.
Seed rate: 20-25 kg/ha for hybrids; 25-30 kg/ha for composites.
Treat seed with Thiram 75 WS @ 2.5 g/kg seed OR Captan 75 WP @ 2.5 g/kg seed.
For stalk rot, treat with Metalaxyl 35 WS @ 2 g/kg seed.
Bio-inoculate with Azospirillum 600 g/ha + PSB 600 g/ha mixed in 50 ml water per kg seed.
Dry treated seeds in shade for 30 minutes before sowing.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing Method
steps: Use seed-cum-fertilizer drill for precise sowing.
Sow 2-3 seeds per hill at 5-7 cm depth.
Spacing: 60 cm between rows × 20 cm between plants.
Apply basal fertilizer in furrow before sowing: N:P:K = 60:60:40 kg/ha.
Cover seeds with soil and firm with foot for proper seed-soil contact.
Thinning: Remove extra seedlings leaving 1 plant per hill at Day 10-12.
Target plant population: 75,000-80,000 plants/ha.

step_4_irrigation_management.title: Irrigation Schedule (Critical for Zaid Maize)
steps: Pre-sowing irrigation: Apply 3-4 days before sowing for uniform germination.
Irrigation 1 (Germination): Day 0-5 — light irrigation immediately after sowing.
Irrigation 2 (Seedling): Day 12-15 — maintain soil moisture at 50-60%.
Irrigation 3 (Vegetative): Day 25-30 — critical for growth.
Irrigation 4 (Tasseling): Day 35-40 — MOST CRITICAL; do not allow water stress.
Irrigation 5 (Silking): Day 45-50 — essential for pollination.
Irrigation 6 (Grain Filling): Day 55-60 — important for grain development.
Irrigation 7 (Dough stage): Day 65-70 — light irrigation only.
Stop irrigation 10-12 days before harvest.
Each irrigation: 50-60 mm water; total 450 mm.
Use drip irrigation or sprinkler for water efficiency (saves 30-40% water).

schedule.fertilizers: DAP 130 kg/ha + MOP 67 kg/ha + Urea 87 kg/ha (N:P:K = 60:60:40 kg/ha)
schedule.method: Apply in furrows below seed level
Apply in furrows beside plants, cover with soil, then irrigate
Improves grain quality and drought tolerance

step_6_weed_management.title: Weed Control
steps: Pre-emergence: Atrazine 50 WP @ 1.5-2 kg/ha within 2-3 days of sowing.
Post-emergence (Day 20-25): Tembotrione 34.4 SC @ 120 ml/ha for broadleaf and grassy weeds.
Alternative post-emergence: 2,4-D 80 WP @ 1 kg/ha for broadleaf weeds.
Manual inter-cultivation at Day 20 and Day 40 using hand hoe.
Earth up plants at Day 30-35 to support stems and cover surface roots.
Avoid deep cultivation near roots.

pests.symptoms: Window pane feeding, sawdust-like frass in whorl, dead heart
pests.control: Emamectin Benzoate 5 SG @ 425 g/ha OR Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC @ 150 ml/ha
pests.method: Apply into whorl during evening hours

pests.symptoms: Dead heart in vegetative stage, bore holes in stem
pests.control: Carbofuran 3G @ 15 kg/ha in whorl at Day 15-20

pests.symptoms: Dead heart in seedling stage (3-7 leaf stage)
pests.control: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL seed treatment @ 5 ml/kg seed
pests.foliar: Dimethoate 30 EC @ 1 litre/ha at first sign

pests.symptoms: Honeydew secretion, sooty mold, stunted growth
pests.control: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 100 ml/ha OR Dimethoate 30 EC @ 500 ml/ha

pests.symptoms: Root damage, lodging
pests.control: Chlorpyriphos 20 EC @ 4 litres/ha soil drench
pests.timing: At planting or early vegetative stage

diseases.symptoms: Long, elliptical, tan lesions on leaves; lesions parallel to veins
diseases.control: Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha OR Propiconazole 25 EC @ 500 ml/ha
diseases.timing: Spray at 10-day intervals; start at first symptom

diseases.control: Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha OR Carbendazim 50 WP @ 500 g/ha
diseases.timing: At disease appearance; repeat after 10 days

diseases.control: Metalaxyl 35 WS seed treatment @ 2 g/kg; foliar Mancozeb 0.25% spray
diseases.timing: Seed treatment + foliar at early stages

diseases.symptoms: Rotting of lower internodes, lodging, pinkish fungal growth
diseases.control: Avoid waterlogging; apply Potash adequately; seed treatment with Thiram
Propiconazole 25 EC @ 500 ml/ha OR Tebuconazole 250 EC @ 500 ml/ha

step_9_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when husk turns brown and black layer formed at kernel base.
Check maturity: Grain moisture 25-30%; kernels hard with no milk.
Remove cobs by hand or use maize harvester.
Husk cobs immediately after harvest.
Sun-dry cobs for 5-7 days on clean tarpaulin to reach 12-14% moisture.
Shell using maize sheller at 500 RPM to avoid grain damage.
Clean grain using seed cleaner to remove chaff and broken kernels.
Expected yield: 6-9 tonnes/ha (hybrid under irrigation).

step_10_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest Management
steps: Dry grain to 12-14% moisture before storage.
Test moisture: Grain should crack when bitten, not flatten.
Grade by kernel size and colour.
Store in moisture-proof metallic bins or gunny bags on wooden pallets.
Treat storage area with Malathion 50 EC @ 5 ml/litre on walls and floor.
Fumigate with Aluminium Phosphide tablets (1 tablet/tonne) in sealed storage for 5 days.
Stack bags 20 cm away from walls for ventilation.
Stover can be used as silage, fodder, or incorporated as green manure.
For seed purpose: Store at 10-12°C, 50-60% RH in sealed containers.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Fodder Sorghum
steps: Select medium to deep loamy soil; pH 6.5–7.5.
Deep plough once to 20-25 cm; cross harrow 2 times for fine tilth.
Apply 5-8 tonnes/ha FYM and mix during final ploughing.
Form ridges and furrows at 45 cm spacing for better irrigation.
Ensure good drainage; sorghum is sensitive to waterlogging.

step_2_seed_treatment_and_sowing.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
steps: Use multi-cut fodder varieties: CSV-21F, HC-308, Pant Chari-5, SSG-59-3.
Seed rate: 40-50 kg/ha for fodder (higher than grain).
Treat with Thiram 75 WS @ 3g/kg seed + Imidacloprid 70 WS @ 7 ml/kg seed.
Row spacing: 30-40 cm for single cut; 45-60 cm for multi-cut.
Sowing depth: 3-4 cm.
Basal: N:P:K = 40:40:20 kg/ha at sowing.

step_3_irrigation_management.title: Irrigation Schedule
steps: Pre-sowing irrigation: Apply 3 days before sowing.
Irrigation 1: At Day 10-12 (seedling establishment).
Irrigation 2: At Day 25-30 (tillering stage) — critical.
Irrigation 3: After each cut (for multi-cut varieties).
Total 3-4 irrigations for single cut; 5-6 irrigations for multi-cut.
Each irrigation: 40-50 mm water.
Stop irrigation 10 days before final harvest.

schedule.fertilizers: DAP 87 kg/ha + Urea 43 kg/ha + MOP 33 kg/ha (N:P:K = 40:40:20)
Urea 65 kg/ha (30 kg N) + KNO3 1% foliar

step_5_weed_management.title: Weed Control
steps: Pre-emergence: Atrazine 50 WP @ 1 kg/ha within 3 days of sowing.
Manual weeding at Day 20 and Day 40.
Inter-cultivation with hand hoe at Day 25-30.
Watch for Striga (witchweed): use resistant varieties if present in field.

pests.control: Chlorpyriphos 20 EC @ 2 litres/ha at whorl stage
Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 100 ml/ha OR Dimethoate 30 EC @ 500 ml/ha
Carbofuran 3G @ 10 kg/ha at sowing

diseases.control: Metalaxyl seed treatment @ 6 g/kg seed; Mancozeb spray 0.25% at 10-day intervals
Propiconazole 25 EC @ 500 ml/ha
Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha
diseases.timing: At disease appearance; repeat after 10 days

step_7_harvesting.title: Harvesting for Fodder
step_7_harvesting.timing: Day 70–75 (50-55 days for single cut; 60-75 days for multi-cut first cut)
steps: Single cut: Harvest at 50% flowering stage (maximum biomass, good quality).
Multi-cut: First cut at 45-50 days after sowing (40-50 cm height).
Subsequent cuts every 25-30 days.
Cut at 10-15 cm above ground to allow regrowth.
Chop fodder into 2-3 cm pieces for better intake by animals.
Expected yield: 35-45 tonnes/ha green fodder (single cut); 60-80 tonnes/ha (multi-cut over season).

step_8_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest Management
steps: Green fodder: Feed fresh within 24-48 hours for best nutrition.
Silage making: Chop at 60-65% moisture; pack in silo; ferment for 30-45 days.
Hay making: Sun-dry for 3-4 days; bale at 15-20% moisture.
Store hay in dry, shaded area on wooden pallets.
For grain purpose: Harvest when grain hard; dry to 12% moisture.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Summer Moong (Green Gram)
steps: Select well-drained sandy loam to loamy soil; pH 6.0–7.5.
Plough once to 15-20 cm; 2 harrowings for fine tilth.
Apply 5-8 tonnes/ha FYM or compost at final ploughing.
Form flat beds or ridges and furrows for better irrigation.
Ensure field is level and weed-free.

step_2_seed_treatment.title: Seed Selection & Treatment
steps: Use short-duration summer varieties: Pusa Vishal, SML-668, SML-832, PDM-139.
Seed rate: 25-30 kg/ha (higher than Kharif due to shorter growing window).
Treat with Carbendazim 50 WP @ 2g/kg seed + Thiram 75 WS @ 2g/kg seed.
Inoculate with Rhizobium (strain TAL 169) @ 200 g/10 kg seed in 10% jaggery solution.
Inoculate with PSB 200 g/10 kg seed simultaneously.
Shade-dry inoculated seed for 30 minutes before sowing.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing Method
steps: Sow at 4-5 cm depth using seed drill or desi plough.
Row spacing: 30 cm × 10 cm plant spacing (target 40-50 plants/sq.m).
2 seeds per hill; thin to 1 plant at Day 10-12.
Basal: P2O5 40 kg/ha + K2O 20 kg/ha + Starter N 15 kg/ha.
Do NOT apply high N (>20 kg/ha) — inhibits nodulation.
Light irrigation immediately after sowing for uniform germination.

step_4_irrigation_management.title: Irrigation Schedule (Critical for Summer Moong)
steps: Irrigation 1: Pre-sowing (3-4 days before sowing).
Irrigation 2: At germination (Day 3-5) — light irrigation.
Irrigation 3: At vegetative stage (Day 15-20).
Irrigation 4: At flowering (Day 25-30) — MOST CRITICAL.
Irrigation 5: At pod filling (Day 40-45) — important for yield.
Each irrigation: 40-50 mm water.
Avoid waterlogging — causes root rot and yellowing.
Stop irrigation 10 days before harvest.

schedule.fertilizers: SSP 250 kg/ha + MOP 33 kg/ha + Urea 22 kg/ha (N:P:K = 15:40:20)
schedule.method: Band placement 5 cm below and 5 cm beside seed

schedule.fertilizers: 2% DAP solution (20 g DAP per litre water) + 0.2% Borax
schedule.method: Foliar application during cool evening hours

schedule.fertilizers: 1% KNO3 spray (improves pod set and grain filling)

step_6_weed_management.title: Weed Control
steps: Pre-emergence: Pendimethalin 30 EC @ 3 litres/ha within 3 days of sowing.
Post-emergence (Day 20-25): Quizalofop-ethyl 5 EC @ 1 litre/ha for grassy weeds.
Manual weeding at Day 20 and Day 35.
Inter-cultivation with hand hoe at Day 25-30 (shallow to avoid root damage).

pests.symptoms: Bright yellow patches; stunted growth; reduced pod set
pests.control: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 100 ml/ha for whitefly; use virus-resistant varieties (SML-668)
pests.timing: At first sign of whitefly; repeat at 10-day intervals

pests.symptoms: Larvae bore into pods; feed on developing grains
pests.control: Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC @ 150 ml/ha at pod initiation

pests.symptoms: Honeydew secretion; sooty mold; stunted growth
pests.control: Dimethoate 30 EC @ 500 ml/ha OR Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 100 ml/ha

pests.symptoms: Yellowing and curling of leaf margins (hopper burn)
pests.control: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 100 ml/ha OR Quinalphos 25 EC @ 1.5 litres/ha

pests.symptoms: Silvery streaks on leaves; flower drop
pests.control: Spinosad 45 SC @ 73 ml/ha OR Fipronil 5 SC @ 600 ml/ha

diseases.symptoms: White powdery growth on leaves; premature defoliation
diseases.control: Wettable Sulphur 80 WP @ 2 kg/ha OR Tebuconazole 250 EC @ 500 ml/ha
diseases.timing: At first sign; repeat after 10 days

diseases.symptoms: Brown spots with grey centre; yellow halo
diseases.control: Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha OR Carbendazim 50 WP @ 500 g/ha
diseases.timing: At disease appearance; repeat after 10-12 days

diseases.symptoms: Sudden wilting; black lesions on taproot
diseases.control: Seed treatment with Trichoderma viride 4g/kg; avoid waterlogging

diseases.symptoms: Water-soaked spots that turn brown; leaf yellowing
diseases.control: Copper Oxychloride 50 WP @ 2.5 kg/ha + Streptocycline 100 ppm
diseases.timing: At first sign; repeat after 10 days

step_8_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when 80% pods turn black/dark brown.
For summer moong, pods mature uniformly; single harvest possible.
Cut plants at ground level using sickle.
Sun-dry plants for 3-5 days on clean surface.
Thresh by beating or using thresher at 400-500 RPM.
Winnow to separate grain from chaff.
Expected yield: 1-1.5 tonnes/ha (summer crop yields lower than Kharif).

step_9_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest Management
steps: Sun-dry grain to 10-11% moisture (grains should break with snap).
Clean using seed cleaner to remove broken seeds and debris.
Store in airtight metallic bins or gunny bags lined with polythene.
Treat storage area with Malathion 50 EC @ 5 ml/litre.
For long-term storage: Fumigate with Aluminium Phosphide (1 tablet/tonne) for 5-7 days.
Add Neem leaf powder @ 1% to stored grain to prevent pulse beetle.
Shelf life: 6-8 months under proper storage conditions.
For seed purpose: Store at 8-10°C, 40-50% RH in sealed containers.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Summer Urad (Black Gram)
steps: Well-drained sandy loam to clay loam; pH 6.0–7.5.
Plough once to 15-20 cm; 2 harrowings.
Apply 5 tonnes/ha FYM or compost.
Form flat beds or ridges and furrows.
Ensure good drainage — Urad sensitive to waterlogging.

step_2_seed_treatment_and_sowing.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
steps: Use short-duration summer varieties: PU-35, PU-31, TAU-1, UH-85.
Seed rate: 25-30 kg/ha.
Treat with Carbendazim 50 WP @ 2g/kg + Thiram 75 WS @ 2g/kg.
Rhizobium (strain CB756) 200 g/10 kg seed + PSB 200 g/10 kg seed.
Row spacing: 30 cm × 10 cm plant spacing.
Sowing depth: 4-5 cm.
Basal: P2O5 40 kg/ha + K2O 20 kg/ha + Starter N 15 kg/ha.

step_3_irrigation_management.title: Irrigation Schedule
steps: Pre-sowing irrigation: 3-4 days before sowing.
Irrigation 1: At germination (Day 3-5).
Irrigation 2: At vegetative stage (Day 15-20).
Irrigation 3: At flowering (Day 25-30) — critical.
Irrigation 4: At pod formation (Day 40-45).
Each irrigation: 40-50 mm.
Stop irrigation 10-12 days before harvest.

schedule.fertilizers: SSP 250 kg/ha + MOP 33 kg/ha + Urea 22 kg/ha

pests.control: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 100 ml/ha; use resistant variety PU-35
Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC @ 150 ml/ha at pod initiation
Dimethoate 30 EC @ 500 ml/ha
Spinosad 45 SC @ 73 ml/ha

diseases.control: Wettable Sulphur 80 WP @ 2 kg/ha OR Tebuconazole 250 EC @ 500 ml/ha
Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha
Trichoderma viride seed treatment; avoid waterlogging

step_6_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when 80% pods turn black.
Cut plants; sun-dry for 3-5 days.
Thresh; winnow.
Expected yield: 0.8-1.3 tonnes/ha.

step_7_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Dry to 10% moisture.
Store in airtight containers with Neem leaf powder.
Fumigate with Aluminium Phosphide for long-term storage.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Summer Sunflower
steps: Well-drained sandy loam to clay loam; pH 6.0–8.0.
Deep plough once to 25-30 cm; harrow 2-3 times.
Apply 8-10 tonnes/ha FYM.
Form fine seedbed; ridges and furrows at 60 cm spacing.

step_2_seed_treatment_and_sowing.title: Seed Selection & Sowing
steps: Use short-duration hybrids: KBSH-53, DRSH-1, LSFH-171, KBSH-44.
Seed rate: 6-8 kg/ha (hybrid); 10-12 kg/ha (OPV).
Treat with Carbendazim 2g/kg + Thiram 2g/kg.
Row spacing: 60 cm; plant spacing: 30 cm.
Sowing depth: 3-5 cm.
Basal: N:P:K = 40:60:40 kg/ha.

step_3_irrigation_management.title: Irrigation Schedule
steps: Pre-sowing irrigation: 3-4 days before sowing.
Irrigation 1: At germination (Day 3-5).
Irrigation 2: At button stage (Day 25-30) — critical.
Irrigation 3: At flowering (Day 40-45) — most critical.
Irrigation 4: At seed filling (Day 60-65).
Each irrigation: 50-60 mm.
Drip irrigation beneficial (saves 30% water).
Stop irrigation 10-12 days before harvest.

schedule.fertilizers: DAP 130 kg/ha + MOP 67 kg/ha + Urea 43 kg/ha (N:P:K = 40:60:40)

pests.symptoms: Larvae feed on florets; webbing on head
pests.control: Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC @ 150 ml/ha at 50% flowering

pests.symptoms: Plants cut at base near ground level
pests.control: Chlorpyriphos 20 EC @ 2 litres/ha soil drench

pests.symptoms: Yellowing; sooty mold
pests.control: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 100 ml/ha

pests.symptoms: Hopper burn; leaf curling
pests.control: Dimethoate 30 EC @ 500 ml/ha

diseases.control: Propiconazole 25 EC @ 500 ml/ha
Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha OR Chlorothalonil 75 WP @ 1.5 kg/ha
diseases.timing: At disease appearance; repeat after 10 days

diseases.control: Wettable Sulphur 80 WP @ 2 kg/ha OR Tebuconazole 250 EC @ 500 ml/ha
Control whitefly vector; rogue infected plants

step_6_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when back of head turns yellow-brown and seeds have black stripes.
Check maturity: Seeds hard; moisture 18-20%.
Cut heads with 20-30 cm stalk using sickle.
Sun-dry heads for 3-5 days on clean tarpaulin.
Thresh by beating or using maize sheller at 400-500 RPM.
Winnow to separate seed from chaff.
Expected yield: 1.5-2.5 tonnes/ha.

step_7_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest Management
steps: Dry seed to 8-10% moisture before storage.
Clean to remove broken seeds and debris.
Store in gunny bags in cool, dry, well-ventilated store.
Fumigate with Aluminium Phosphide (1 tablet/tonne) for 5-7 days.
Oil content: 40-45%; high-quality edible oil.
Sunflower meal (oil cake) used as cattle feed (30-35% protein).

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Early Summer Sesame (Til)
steps: Well-drained sandy loam or red loamy soil; pH 5.5–8.0.
Plough once to 20 cm; 2-3 harrowings for fine tilth.
Apply 5-8 tonnes/ha FYM at final ploughing.
Form ridges and furrows or flat beds; sesame needs good drainage.
Ensure weed-free seedbed (sesame seeds are very small).

step_2_seed_treatment_and_sowing.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
steps: Use short-duration varieties: GT-10, RT-127, GT-4, TKG-22.
Seed rate: 5-6 kg/ha (hybrid); 6-8 kg/ha (varieties).
Treat with Agrosan GN @ 2g/kg seed OR Thiram 3g/kg.
Mix seed with dry sand 1:4 ratio for uniform sowing (very small seeds).
Row spacing: 30-45 cm; depth: 2-3 cm.
Thin to 10-15 cm plant spacing at Day 10-12.
Basal: N:P:K = 20:20:10 kg/ha.

step_3_irrigation_management.title: Irrigation Schedule
steps: Pre-sowing irrigation: 2-3 days before sowing.
Irrigation 1: At germination (Day 3-5) — light irrigation.
Irrigation 2: At vegetative stage (Day 15-20).
Irrigation 3: At flowering (Day 25-30) — critical.
Irrigation 4: At capsule formation (Day 45-50).
Each irrigation: 40-50 mm.
Avoid waterlogging — causes root rot (Phytophthora).
Stop irrigation 15 days before harvest.

schedule.fertilizers: SSP 125 kg/ha + MOP 17 kg/ha + Urea 43 kg/ha

pests.symptoms: Galls on leaves, stem, and capsules
pests.control: Dimethoate 30 EC @ 1 litre/ha at flowering; destroy galled capsules

pests.symptoms: Leaves rolled and webbed together; defoliation
pests.control: Quinalphos 25 EC @ 1.5 litres/ha OR Chlorpyriphos 20 EC @ 2 litres/ha

pests.symptoms: Honeydew secretion; sooty mold
pests.control: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 100 ml/ha

pests.symptoms: Yellowing; virus vector
pests.control: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 100 ml/ha + Yellow sticky traps

diseases.control: Seed treatment with Metalaxyl 35 WS @ 2g/kg; improve drainage; drench Metalaxyl + Mancozeb 72 WP @ 3 g/litre
Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha; 2-3 sprays at 10-day intervals
Wettable Sulphur 80 WP @ 2 kg/ha OR Tebuconazole 250 EC @ 500 ml/ha
Copper Oxychloride 50 WP @ 2.5 kg/ha + Streptocycline 100 ppm

step_6_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when lower capsules begin to yellow-brown (before shattering).
Cut plants at base with sickle early morning (less shattering).
Bundle plants and stand upright on tarpaulin; shake daily to collect shattered seeds.
Sun-dry bundles for 5-7 days.
Thresh by beating bundles on tarpaulin.
Winnow to separate seed from chaff.
Expected yield: 0.5-0.8 tonnes/ha (summer crop).

step_7_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest Management
steps: Dry seed to 6-8% moisture (high oil content — prone to rancidity).
Clean to remove chaff and debris.
Store in airtight containers in cool, dark place.
Add neem leaf powder or silica gel packets to absorb moisture.
Oil extraction: Cold pressing preferred for quality sesame oil.
Sesame oil content: 45-50% of seed weight.
Sesame meal used as high-protein cattle feed.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Watermelon
steps: Sandy loam, well drained; pH 6.0–7.0.
River bed cultivation (sandy silt) is ideal for summer watermelon.
Deep plough once to 25-30 cm; harrow 2-3 times.
Apply 15-20 tonnes/ha FYM; incorporate.
Prepare pits 60×60×45 cm at 3 m × 2 m spacing for vine-type varieties.

step_2_seed_treatment_and_sowing.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
steps: Use summer-adapted hybrids: Sugar Baby, Asahi Yamato, Arka Manik, Madhuri.
Seed rate: 3-4 kg/ha.
Treat seed with Thiram 3g/kg + Carbendazim 1g/kg.
Soak seeds in water for 8-12 hours before sowing.
Fill pits with FYM 10 kg + SSP 60 g + MOP 40 g per pit.
Sow 3 seeds per pit at 2-3 cm depth.
Thin to 2 plants per pit at Day 10-12.

step_3_irrigation_management.title: Irrigation Schedule
steps: Irrigation 1: Immediately after sowing for germination.
Irrigation 2: At vegetative stage (Day 15-20).
Irrigation 3: At flowering (Day 30-35) — critical.
Irrigation 4: At fruit set (Day 40-45).
Irrigation 5: At fruit development (Day 55-60).
Each irrigation: 40-50 mm.
Reduce irrigation during fruit ripening (last 15 days) for better sweetness.
Avoid waterlogging — causes fruit rot.

schedule.fertilizers: FYM 10 kg/pit + SSP 60 g/pit + MOP 40 g/pit
Urea 65 kg/ha (30 kg N) + KNO3 1% foliar
KNO3 1% spray at fruit ripening stage

step_5_vine_management.title: Vine Management
steps: Train vines in desired direction (away from irrigation channels).
After fruit set, retain 2-3 fruits per vine; remove remaining to get large fruits.
Place dry straw or plastic mulch under fruit to prevent soil contact (rot prevention).
Pinch the growing tip after 10-12 leaves to encourage lateral branches.

pests.symptoms: Puncture marks on fruit; maggots inside fruit; fruit rotting
pests.control: Malathion 50 EC bait (50 ml + 500 ml molasses + 450 ml water) in traps; DDVP 76 EC @ 1 ml/litre spray
pests.timing: At fruit set and development; repeat every 7-10 days
pests.prevention: Cover young fruits with paper bags

pests.symptoms: Skeletonized leaves; defoliation
pests.control: Carbaryl 50 WP @ 2 kg/ha OR Chlorpyriphos 20 EC @ 2 litres/ha

pests.symptoms: Honeydew secretion; sooty mold
pests.control: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 100 ml/ha

pests.symptoms: Silvery streaks on leaves; fruit scarring
pests.control: Spinosad 45 SC @ 73 ml/ha OR Fipronil 5 SC @ 600 ml/ha

diseases.symptoms: Dark sunken lesions on fruit; leaf spots
diseases.control: Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha + Carbendazim 50 WP @ 500 g/ha; spray at 7-10 day intervals
Trifloxystrobin 25% + Tebuconazole 50% WG @ 400 g/ha OR Wettable Sulphur @ 2 kg/ha

diseases.symptoms: Yellow angular spots; purplish down on leaf underside
diseases.control: Metalaxyl + Mancozeb 72 WP @ 3 g/litre OR Chlorothalonil 75 WP @ 1.5 kg/ha

diseases.symptoms: Watery rot of fruit touching soil
diseases.control: Metalaxyl + Mancozeb 72 WP drench; avoid fruit-soil contact; use mulch

diseases.symptoms: Mottled leaves; stunted growth; deformed fruit
diseases.control: Control aphid vectors with Imidacloprid; remove infected plants
diseases.prevention: Use virus-free seed; reflective mulch to repel aphids

step_7_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Signs of maturity: Dull sound on tapping (not metallic).
Yellow ground spot where fruit rests on soil.
Dry, brown tendril near fruit.
Cut with 5 cm stalk using sharp knife.
Handle carefully — no dropping or bruising.
Grade by weight: Extra Large > 8 kg; Large 6-8 kg; Medium 4-6 kg; Small < 4 kg.
Expected yield: 30-50 tonnes/ha.

step_8_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest Management
steps: Pre-cool to 10-15°C within 4 hours of harvest.
Store at 10-15°C, 90% RH for 2-3 weeks.
Do not store below 10°C (chilling injury).
Transport in padded crates to prevent bruising.
Market fresh or process into juice, fruit salad.
Shelf life at room temperature: 5-7 days.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Muskmelon
steps: Sandy loam; pH 6.0–7.5; well drained.
Plough once to 25 cm; harrow 2-3 times.
Apply 15-20 tonnes/ha FYM.
Form pits 60×60×30 cm at 2.5 m × 1.5 m spacing.
Fill pits with FYM + top soil + SSP 50 g + MOP 30 g per pit.

step_2_seed_treatment_and_sowing.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
steps: Use summer varieties: Arka Jeet, Pusa Madhuras, Hara Madhu, Durgapura Madhu.
Seed rate: 2-3 kg/ha.
Treat with Thiram 3g/kg seed.
Soak seeds in water for 8 hours before sowing.
Sow 3 seeds per pit at 2-3 cm depth.
Thin to 2 plants per pit at Day 10-12.

step_3_irrigation_and_fertilizer.title: Irrigation & Fertilizer
irrigation_steps: Weekly irrigation during vegetative and flowering stage.
Reduce irrigation at 2 weeks before harvest to improve sugar content.

fertilizer_schedule.fertilizers: Urea 43 kg/ha (20 kg N) + KNO3 0.5% foliar for sweetness

pests.control: Malathion bait traps; DDVP 76 EC @ 1 ml/litre spray
Carbaryl 50 WP @ 2 kg/ha
Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 100 ml/ha

diseases.control: Trifloxystrobin + Tebuconazole WG @ 400 g/ha
Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha
Avoid soil contact; Mancozeb spray; use mulch
Mancozeb + Carbendazim spray

step_5_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Signs of maturity: Sweet aroma; easy separation from vine (slip stage).
Yellow-orange skin colour for most varieties.
Harvest in cool morning hours.
Cut with 2-3 cm stalk.
Expected yield: 15-25 tonnes/ha.

step_6_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Store at 5-10°C; shelf life 2-3 weeks.
Highly perishable — sell within 5-7 days at ambient.
Process into juice, jam, or dried slices.
Handle gently — bruising reduces shelf life.

step_1_soil_preparation_and_sowing.title: Soil Preparation & Sowing
steps: Sandy loam, well-drained; pH 6.0–7.0.
Plough once; harrow 2 times.
Apply 15 tonnes/ha FYM.
Form raised beds 1 m wide or pits 45×45 cm at 1.5 m × 1 m spacing.
Use varieties: Pusa Sanyog, Poinsette, Gypsy, Summer Green.
Seed rate: 3-4 kg/ha.
Treat seed with Thiram 3g/kg.
Sow 2-3 seeds per pit at 2-3 cm depth.

step_2_irrigation_and_fertilizer.title: Irrigation & Fertilizer
irrigation_steps: Critical at flowering and fruit development.
Avoid water stress — causes bitterness in fruits.

pests.control: DDVP 1 ml/litre + Malathion bait traps
Carbaryl 50 WP @ 2 kg/ha
Imidacloprid 100 ml/ha

diseases.control: Metalaxyl + Mancozeb 72 WP @ 3 g/litre
Wettable Sulphur @ 2 kg/ha
Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha

step_4_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when fruits are 15-20 cm long (depending on variety).
Pick every 2-3 days during peak season.
Cut fruit with small stalk using sharp knife.
Expected yield: 15-25 tonnes/ha.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Bitter Gourd (Karela)
steps: Sandy loam, well-drained; pH 6.0–7.0.
Plough once; harrow 2-3 times.
Apply 15-20 tonnes/ha FYM.
Pits 45×45×45 cm at 2 m × 1.5 m spacing.

step_2_seed_treatment_and_sowing.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
steps: Use varieties: Pusa Vishesh, Kashi Mayuri, Priya, Arka Harit.
Seed rate: 5-6 kg/ha.
Nick seeds on flat side (opposite to pointed end) for better germination.
Soak in water for 12 hours before sowing.
Treat with Thiram 3g/kg seed.
Sow 2 seeds per pit at 2-3 cm depth.

step_3_training_and_fertilizer.title: Training & Fertilizer
training_steps: Erect pandal/trellis at 1.5-2 m height with GI wire and bamboo.
Train vines onto trellis from Day 15-20.
Pinch tip of main stem at Day 20-25 to encourage lateral branches.

fertilizer_schedule.fertilizers: FYM 5 kg + SSP 50 g + MOP 30 g per pit

pests.control: DDVP 1 ml/litre; protein bait traps
Chlorpyriphos 20 EC @ 2 litres/ha
Dimethoate 30 EC @ 500 ml/ha

diseases.control: Wettable Sulphur @ 2 kg/ha
Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha
Mancozeb + Carbendazim combined spray

step_5_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest tender fruits at 5-7 cm length for better taste.
Do not allow yellow or red colouring — fruits become extremely bitter.
Cut fruit with small stalk.
Expected yield: 10-20 tonnes/ha.

step_1_soil_preparation_and_sowing.title: Soil Prep & Sowing for Bottle Gourd (Lauki)
steps: Sandy loam; pH 6.0–7.0; well drained.
Pits 60×60×30 cm at 2.5 m × 1.5 m spacing.
Fill pits with FYM 5 kg + SSP 50 g + MOP 30 g per pit.
Use varieties: Pusa Santushti, Pusa Naveen, Arka Bahar.
Seed rate: 4-5 kg/ha.
Soak seeds in water for 12 hours.
Sow 2-3 seeds per pit; thin to 2 plants.

step_2_training_and_fertilizer.title: Training & Fertilizer
training_steps: Train vines onto trellis from Day 15-20.

step_3_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest tender fruits at 15-25 cm length.
Do not allow over-maturity — fruits become woody and inedible.
Expected yield: 30-50 tonnes/ha.

step_1_soil_preparation_and_sowing.title: Soil Prep & Sowing for Ridge Gourd (Tori)
steps: Sandy loam; pH 6.0–7.0.
Pits 45×45×30 cm at 2.5 m × 1.5 m spacing.
Fill with FYM 5 kg + SSP 50 g + MOP 30 g per pit.
Use varieties: Pusa Nasdar, Arka Sujata, Kashi Ganga.
Seed rate: 4-5 kg/ha.
Sow 2-3 seeds per pit; thin to 2 plants.

step_2_irrigation_and_harvesting.title: Irrigation & Harvesting
irrigation_steps: Critical at flowering and fruit development.
harvesting_steps: Harvest at 10-15 cm length; ridges clearly defined.
Do not allow over-maturity — fruits become fibrous.

step_1_soil_preparation_and_sowing.title: Soil Prep & Sowing for Pumpkin (Kaddu)
steps: Sandy loam to loam; pH 6.0–7.0.
Pits 60×60×45 cm at 3 m × 3 m spacing.
Fill with FYM 10 kg + SSP 60 g + MOP 40 g per pit.
Use varieties: Pusa Vishwas, Arka Chandan, Ambili.
Seed rate: 3-4 kg/ha.
Sow 3 seeds per pit; thin to 2 plants.

step_2_fertilizer_and_irrigation.title: Fertilizer & Irrigation
irrigation_steps: Reduce irrigation during fruit ripening (last 15-20 days).

step_3_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: For vegetable use: harvest immature green fruits at Day 60-70.
For storage: harvest mature fruits when skin turns orange/yellow and stem dries.
Cut with 5 cm stalk attached.
Expected yield: 20-35 tonnes/ha.

step_1_soil_preparation_and_sowing.title: Soil Prep & Sowing for Fodder Cowpea
steps: Sandy loam; pH 6.0–7.5.
Plough once; harrow 2 times.
Apply 5-6 tonnes/ha FYM.
Use multi-cut fodder varieties: Russian Giant, UPC-4200, CL-367.
Seed rate: 40-50 kg/ha (higher for fodder).
Row spacing: 25-30 cm; depth 3-4 cm.
Treat seed with Rhizobium @ 200 g/10 kg seed.

step_2_fertilizer_and_irrigation.title: Fertilizer & Irrigation

fertilizer_schedule.fertilizers: Urea 43 kg/ha (20 kg N) for regrowth

irrigation_steps: Critical at each regrowth after cutting.

step_3_harvesting.title: Harvesting for Fodder
step_3_harvesting.timing: Day 55–60 (first cut); subsequent cuts every 25-30 days
steps: First cut at 50-55 days after sowing (40-50 cm height).
Cut at 10 cm above ground for regrowth.
Chop into 2-3 cm pieces for feeding.
Expected yield: 25-30 tonnes/ha green fodder.

step_1_sowing_and_fertilizer.title: Sowing & Fertilizer for Fodder Maize
steps: Use varieties: J-1006, African Tall, Vijay Composite.
Seed rate: 60-70 kg/ha (higher for fodder).
Row spacing: 30-40 cm; depth 4-5 cm.
Basal: N:P:K = 60:40:30 kg/ha.
Top dressing: Urea 87 kg/ha at Day 25-30.

step_2_harvesting.title: Harvesting for Fodder
steps: Harvest at 50% flowering for best quality (high protein, low fibre).
Cut at ground level.
Chop into 2-3 cm pieces for silage or fresh feeding.
Expected yield: 35-45 tonnes/ha green fodder.

step_1_sowing_and_fertilizer.title: Sowing & Fertilizer for Fodder Sorghum
steps: Use multi-cut varieties: CSV-21F, HC-308, Pant Chari-5.
Seed rate: 50-60 kg/ha (higher for fodder).
Row spacing: 30-40 cm; depth 3-4 cm.
Basal: N:P:K = 50:40:30 kg/ha.
After each cut: Urea 65 kg/ha (30 kg N) for regrowth.

step_2_harvesting.title: Harvesting for Fodder
step_2_harvesting.timing: Day 60–70 (first cut); subsequent cuts every 25-30 days
steps: First cut at 50-55 days after sowing.
Cut at 10-15 cm above ground.
Expected yield: 40-50 tonnes/ha green fodder (multi-cut).
